quarantine.mr_name: SAM header TAG:VALUE pair ordering invariance
quarantine.oracle: Parsed SAM header metadata should be identical regardless of intra-line TAG:VALUE pair order

evidence.quote: Within each (non- @CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM optional tag field ordering invariance
quarantine.oracle: Parsed alignment records should have identical optional field dictionaries regardless of field order

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.oracle: Alignment position and query consumption should be invariant under splitting or merging adjacent identical CIGAR operations

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p134
evidence.quote: * Adjacent CIGAR operations should be different.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The spec says 'should be different' (ADVISORY) not 'must be different', so parsers MAY accept adjacent identical operations

quarantine.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject (raise error/crash) or at minimum flag as invalid any SAM record where sum of query-consuming CIGAR operators (M, I, S, =, X) does not equal len(SEQ) when SEQ != '*'. Silent acceptance or silent record-drop is a conformance bug.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p137
evidence.quote: * If all segments in a template are unmapped, their RNAME should be set as `*' and POS as 0.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

quarantine.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject (raise error/crash) or at minimum flag as invalid any SAM record where flag 0x4 (segment unmapped) is set while RNAME != '*' and POS > 0. The spec states that when 0x4 is set, no assumptions can be made about RNAME, POS, CIGAR, MAPQ, and bits 0x2, 0x100, and 0x800, but having mapped coordinates with unmapped flag is contradictory.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p137
evidence.quote: * If all segments in a template are unmapped, their RNAME should be set as `*' and POS as 0.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The spec uses 'should' not 'must' for this particular recommendation, but the contradiction between mapped coordinates and unmapped flag is logically invalid

quarantine.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject (raise error/crash) or at minimum flag as invalid any SAM record where an optional tag's type character is not one of the legal characters A, i, f, Z, H, B (plus numeric subtypes inside B). Illegal type characters like 'X' must cause rejection.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p130
evidence.quote: * The @HD line should be present, with either the SO tag or the GO tag (but not both) specified.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The evidence chunk discusses header lines not optional tags, but the SAMtags specification (referenced in the prompt) defines the legal type characters

quarantine.mr_name: TLEN sign consistency violation for paired reads
quarantine.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject (raise error/crash) or at minimum flag as invalid when paired reads of a template have TLEN values with the same sign instead of opposite signs. The two reads of a template MUST carry equal-magnitude opposite-signed TLEN values.

evidence.quote: Bit 0x4 is the only reliable place to tell whether the read is unmapped. If 0x4 is set, no assumptions can be made about RNAME, POS, CIGAR, MAPQ, and bits 0x2, 0x100, and 0x800.

ambiguity_flags: The evidence chunk discusses flag bits not TLEN, but the SAM specification §1.4 defines the TLEN sign consistency rule

quarantine.mr_name: SAM optional tag field ordering round-trip invariance
quarantine.oracle: Parsed canonical content must be identical after shuffling optional tag order and BAM round-trip

evidence.quote:
Predefined tags are described in the separate  Sequence Alignment/Map
Optional Fields Specification.See
http://samtools.github.io/hts-specs/SAMtags.pdf SAMtags.pdf
at https://github.com/samtools/hts-specs.
See that document for details of existing standard tag fields and conventions
around creating new tags that may be of general interest.
Tags starting with ` X', ` Y' or ` Z' and tags containing
lowercase letters in either position are reserved for local use and will
not be formally defined in any future version of these specifications.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM CIGAR operator splitting/merging round-trip invariance
quarantine.oracle: Parsed canonical content must be identical after splitting/merging adjacent identical CIGAR operators and BAM round-trip

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p134
evidence.quote: * Adjacent CIGAR operations should be different.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The spec uses 'should' (ADVISORY) rather than 'must', but canonical normalizer merges adjacent identical operators, making this effectively invariant for round-trip

quarantine.oracle: Parsed canonical content must be identical after SAM→BAM→SAM round-trip through samtools

evidence.quote:
[SAM v1 — The BAM Format Specification] The BAM format
BAM is compressed in the BGZF format. All multi-byte numbers in BAM are
little-endian, regardless of the machine endianness. The format is
formally described in the following table where values in brackets are
the default when the corresponding information is not available; an
underlined word in uppercase denotes a field in the SAM format.

underlined word in uppercase denotes a field in the SAM format.
